content:
I think the last time I looked onto the E100e. that I'm currently unable to write in rust. I think I could manage e1000, except one very important part. This MODULE_DEVICE_TABLE macro I'm unable to write in rust. maybe somebody can help me to integrate that into tho module proc_macro.
https://elixir.bootlin.com/linux/latest/source/include/linux/module.h#L237
If you are talking about the symbol name concatenation you will need a proc macro for that. Either implement everything as a proc macro or (harder) vendor and use the paste crate.
Oh, and nightly has concat_ident!, but I don't think it is on track for stabilization any time soon.
I'm more missing the other halft of that Define. So at least until now, I did not understand that macro conceptually.

Conceptually I now understand how to do it. I can do it on my own, but this would mean that every driver had to do it themselves. Maybe We can come up with a method to ad it to the module macro. My own method would be to add #[extern_name = ...] infront of my static variable

Miguel and Wedson seem to be unwilling to have a new proc macro or add new features to existing proc macros for now, probably it can be added later after we've upstreamed. Probably for now you can add #[extern_name = ...] and we can revisit this later when there are more users that need this macro?
Yeah, I think that would be best for now -- then when we have several users, we can argue easily for having more proc macros etc.
